name: Swiggy Order Service — Bulkhead Feature Isolation
scenario: Swiggy's order service handles live tracking (50 concurrent max), restaurant search (30 max), and payment processing (20 max) in parallel. Without bulkheads, a surge in tracking requests starves payment threads. Add @Bulkhead isolation to each feature.

core.description: application.yml: 3 bulkhead configs — order-tracking (max=50, wait=200ms), restaurant-search (max=30, wait=100ms), payment-processing (max=20, wait=500ms).
SwiggyOrderService: three @Bulkhead-annotated methods with appropriate fallbacks. Tracking: cached data. Search: pre-loaded top restaurants. Payment: QUEUED status.
BulkheadSaturationMonitor @Service: event listeners on all three bulkheads. @Scheduled every minute: log rejection counts, alert if > 50 rejections/min.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): payment works normally, tracking fallback returns cached data, search fallback returns default list. All 3 pass.
README: explains bulkhead analogy (ship compartments) and why isolation matters.

bonus.description: Add THREADPOOL bulkhead for a 4th feature: report generation (max 5 threads, returns CompletableFuture). Test async report generation.
Full stack: add @CircuitBreaker inside each @Bulkhead. Both fallbacks. Test: circuit opens, then bulkhead full.
BulkheadDashboard @RestController: /admin/bulkheads returns all bulkhead names, available slots, utilization %. MockMvc test.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. BulkheadFullException: import io.github.resilience4j.bulkhead.BulkheadFullException — use as last fallback parameter (or Exception). THREADPOOL bulkhead requires CompletableFuture return type. @Bulkhead OUTER when combined with @CircuitBreaker: concurrent slot check happens before circuit check. maxWaitDuration: how long to wait for a slot — 0ms = fail immediately, longer = queue briefly. Separate bulkhead instances are completely independent: filling tracking slots has zero effect on payment slots.
